content.full_text:
PPPC
Nov 13 10 27 AM '90

City Of
Encinitas

November 8, 1990


Technical Assistance Committee
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95804-0814

I am writing to request advice in writing on the following
situation. A man has just been elected, November 6, as a member
of the Encinitas City Council. He is an insurance agent and I have
insurance on my mobile home with him. He ran (unsuccessfully) for
City Council two years ago and prior to that election I called the
FPPC to see if there would be any conflict if he should be elected.
In the telephone call I was told that there would only be a
conflict if he were to be involved in making a decision on my
salary. However, since he was not elected at that time I did not
request a written opinion.

I need a written response very quickly, please, because the new
Council will probably be seated the last week in November and I am
scheduled for Council review of my contract and salary either that
same week or in early December.

Facts of note are these: 1). My insurance policy names his wife as
the agent, although he was the person who actually asked me for the
business. 2) I have heard that the Council Member-elect has stated
he intends to turn the insurance business over to his wife while
he is a Council Member to avoid any potential conflicts.

I would appreciate your prompt reply as I am sure that neither he
nor I wants to do anything other than follow the letter, spirit and
intent of the law.


Very truly yours,

[signature]
E/Jane Pool
City Clerk


527 Encinitas Boulevard, Encinitas, California 92024     TEL 619-944-5050 / FAX 619-632-9836     [recycled paper symbol]

sections.question: I am writing to request advice in writing on the following situation. A man has just been elected, November 6, as a member of the Encinitas City Council. He is an insurance agent and I have insurance on my mobile home with him. He ran (unsuccessfully) for city Council two years ago and prior to that election I called the FPPC to see if there would be any conflict if he should be elected. In the telephone call I was told that there would only be a conflict if he were to be involved in making a decision on my salary. However, since he was not elected at that time I did not request a written opinion. I need a written response very quickly, please, because the new Council will probably be seated the last week in November and I am scheduled for Council review of my contract and salary either that same week or in early December. Facts of note are these: 1). My insurance policy names his wife as the agent, although he was the person who actually asked me for the business. 2) I have heard that the Council Member-elect has stated he intends to turn the insurance business over to his wife while he is a council Member to avoid any potential conflicts.
sections.question_synthetic: Does a city clerk's existing personal insurance relationship with a newly elected council member create a conflict of interest under FPPC law, particularly when the council member intends to transfer the insurance business to his wife and the clerk will soon have their contract and salary reviewed by the council?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or advice letter content is present. Document is a formal written request for technical assistance from the FPPC's Technical Assistance Committee dated November 8, 1990.

embedding.summary: Jane Pool, City Clerk of Encinitas, requests written FPPC advice regarding whether her existing insurance relationship with a newly elected council member creates a conflict of interest, given that her contract and salary will be reviewed by the council shortly after the member is seated.
